title: Robustly Roasted Polish Lamb Stew
description: A hearty and romantic Polish dish featuring lamb, green cardamom pods, and a blend of spices, cooked to perfection in a Dutch oven. This low-glycemic-friendly stew is perfect for a cold winter's night.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb pieces with salt, black pepper, and dried thyme.
Grill the lamb pieces for 5 minutes per side, or until browned.
Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in the Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
Add the crushed green cardamom pods and cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
Add the chopped carrots and celery and cook for 5 minutes, or until tender.
Add the browned lamb pieces, lamb broth, and tomato paste to the Dutch oven.
Bring the stew to a boil, then cover and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
